fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Illinois Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity illinois, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to illinois, speech-language pathologist compact illinois, illinois speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Division of Professional Regulation — Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)
board.mailing_address: 320 W. Washington St., 3rd Floor, Springfield, IL 62786
board.physical_address: 320 W. Washington St., 3rd Floor, Springfield, IL 62786

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Illinois. Requires a master's degree, completion of the Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY), and a passing Praxis score.

license_types.title: Temporary SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY) or to out-of-state SLPs awaiting Illinois licensure. Application fee: $75. Allows supervised practice while completing remaining requirements.

steps.title: Complete Education and Clinical Experience
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or doctorate in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation (CAA) and complete supervised clinical experience.

steps.title: Pass the Examination
steps.description: Pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology administered by the Educational Testing Service (ETS).

steps.title: Apply for Licensure
steps.description: Submit an application for licensure to the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR) along with all required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: From an IDFPR-approved or ASHA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
Documentation of completed CFY signed by supervising licensed SLP.
Criminal background check as required by IDFPR.

telepractice_text: Illinois permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Illinois SLP license to provide services to clients located in Illinois. Illinois does not currently participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. All telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person services.
provisional_text: Illinois issues a Temporary SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY). The temporary license costs $75 and permits supervised practice while the CFY is being completed. Out-of-state SLPs awaiting Illinois licensure may also apply for the temporary license.
reciprocity_text: Illinois offers licensure by endorsement for SLPs licensed in other states. The endorsement application fee is $100. Applicants must demonstrate equivalent education, examination, and experience. ASHA CCC-SLP holders may have the review process streamlined but must still submit a full Illinois application.

faqs.question: How much does an Illinois SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is $90 (by examination) or $100 (by endorsement). A Temporary SLP License costs $75. Biennial renewal is $100, due by October 31 of odd-numbered years.

faqs.question: Do I need a PEL to work as an SLP in Illinois schools?
faqs.answer: Yes. SLPs working in Illinois public schools must hold a Professional Educator License (PEL) from the Illinois State Board of Education in addition to the IDFPR professional SLP license.

faqs.question: What continuing education is required for Illinois SLP renewal?
faqs.answer: Illinois requires 20 hours of CE every two years, including 1 hour of ethics, 1 hour of sexual harassment training, 1 hour of implicit bias awareness, 1 hour of dementia recognition training (every 6 years), and 1 hour of cultural competency training (effective January 1, 2025).

faqs.question: Does Illinois have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Illinois?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Illinois offers licensure by endorsement for SLPs licensed in other states. The endorsement application fee is $100. Applicants must demonstrate equivalent education, examination, and experience. ASHA CCC-SLP holders may have the review process streamlined but must still submit a full Illinois application.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Illinois
hero.dek: Illinois Speech-Language Pathologist License requires a master's degree, completion of supervised clinical experience, and passing the national examination.
